title: Stratasys Rises As Markforged Deal Boosts Manufacturing, Defense Expansion Plans
author: Akanksha Bakshi
description: Stratasys announced its acquisition of MarkForged for $42.5 million, expected to close in H2 2026. The deal aims to enhance capabilities in aerospace and defense sectors, generate cross-sale opportunities, and achieve cost synergies. The stock rose on the announcement, trading above its 20-day and 50-day moving averages with a bullish technical outlook.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition of MarkForged is strategically significant, expected to enhance distribution channels, expand capabilities in high-demand sectors (aerospace and defense), generate cross-sale opportunities, and be accretive to adjusted EBITDA. The stock showed positive price movement and bullish technical indicators with a Buy consensus rating and $11 price target.

title: Additive Manufacturing in Aerospace Market Size to Reach USD 44.96 Billion by 2035, Driven by Rising Demand for Lightweight and Fuel-Efficient Aircraft Components – SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global additive manufacturing in aerospace market is projected to grow from USD 8.75 billion in 2025 to USD 44.96 billion by 2035, expanding at a CAGR of 17.79%. Growth is driven by demand for lightweight, fuel-efficient aircraft components, rising aircraft production, and increased adoption of metal additive manufacturing for structural and engine parts. The U.S. market alone is expected to reach USD 10.48 billion by 2033.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company expanded its aerospace-qualified metal additive manufacturing portfolio in February 2024 with enhanced titanium and aluminum alloy solutions for flight-critical components, demonstrating active market participation and innovation.
Introduced upgraded aerospace-focused polymer printing platform in May 2024 with enhanced build speed and material performance, showing continued product development and market engagement.
Listed as major market player in the rapidly growing aerospace additive manufacturing sector with strong market tailwinds and industry consolidation.
Major aerospace manufacturer benefiting from industry shift toward additive manufacturing for production parts and component optimization.
Major aerospace manufacturer benefiting from industry shift toward additive manufacturing for production parts and component optimization.
Leading commercial aircraft manufacturer positioned to benefit from additive manufacturing adoption for lightweight, fuel-efficient components.

title: TCT Asia 2026 Opens Registration: Welcoming the Global Additive Manufacturing Community to Shanghai
author: Globe Newswire
description: TCT Asia 2026, the leading additive manufacturing event in Asia-Pacific, has opened free visitor registration for its March 17-19 event in Shanghai. The event will span 55,000+ m² across two halls featuring over 550 exhibitors, including global leaders and Chinese innovators in metal and polymer 3D printing technologies, alongside a revamped conference format with expert sessions and professional certification courses.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Highlighted as a global titan in the metal AM ecosystem, suggesting competitive positioning and technological advancement in industrial additive manufacturing.
Featured among established international giants in metal AM, indicating recognition as a key player in the additive manufacturing supply chain.
Listed as a major player in polymer and innovation segment, demonstrating continued market relevance in non-metal 3D printing technologies.
Included among major players in the polymer and innovation hall, indicating active participation in the growing additive manufacturing market.

title: Stratasys Q3 Revenue Growth Driven By Strong Customer Engagement
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Stratasys reported Q3 revenue of $137.0 million, beating analyst estimates, with improved net income and maintained positive outlook for annual performance despite slight margin decline.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company exceeded revenue and earnings expectations, showed improved net income, maintained annual guidance, and demonstrated strong customer engagement across manufacturing verticals

title: Saudi Arabia 3D Printing Medical Devices Market Review 2018-2024 and Forecast 2025-2034 Featuring Philips, GE Healthcare, Nikon SLM Solutions, and Stratasys
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Saudi Arabia's 3D printing medical devices market is projected to grow from $52.09 million in 2024 to $208.91 million by 2034, driven by government support, healthcare digitization, and demand for personalized medical solutions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned as a key player in advancing healthcare precision through 3D printing technologies
Recognized as a leading company driving innovation in medical device 3D printing market
Listed among key market players without specific performance indicators
